race.name: Tour de Gaspé
race.tagline: A multi-day expedition across Quebec's Gaspé Peninsula — 150 kilometers of classic skiing through remote boreal wilderness between the St. Lawrence and the Chic-Choc Mountains.

climate.description: The Gaspé Peninsula experiences cold maritime winters with heavy snowfall from the Gulf of St. Lawrence. Temperatures typically -20°C to -5°C.
challenges: Remote wilderness with limited support access
Maritime storms can bring whiteout conditions
Multi-day exposure to cold requires expedition preparation
Variable trail conditions in backcountry sections

course.primary: Multi-day stage race traversing the Gaspé Peninsula through boreal forest, with views of the Chic-Choc Mountains and the Gulf of St. Lawrence.
course.surface: Mixed groomed trails and backcountry tracks
course.grooming: Variable — groomed sections and expedition-style backcountry
features: Multi-day expedition format across the Gaspé Peninsula
Views of the Gulf of St. Lawrence
One of the few multi-day Nordic ski expeditions in North America

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Unique multi-day expedition format across the Gaspé Peninsula. Exceptional scenery and adventure value.

history.summary: The Tour de Gaspé is a multi-day Nordic ski expedition traversing Quebec's remote Gaspé Peninsula. One of the few stage-format Nordic events in North America, it combines competitive skiing with wilderness adventure through the Chic-Choc Mountains.
notable_facts: One of the few multi-day Nordic ski expeditions in North America
Traverses the remote Gaspé Peninsula in eastern Quebec
The Chic-Choc Mountains are the highest peaks in southern Quebec
Expedition format attracts adventure-oriented Nordic skiers

youtube_data.search_query: Tour de Gaspé cross country ski race Gaspé Peninsula

videos.title: Virtual Cross Country Skiing in Gaspésie, Quebec - Nordic Skiing
videos.description:
I'm used to run but here I'm skiing so why not doing a virtual glide instead of virtual run :) This trail is a golf course near the mountains of Carleton-sur-mer in Gaspésie (Gaspe). We had 3 days of hot weather so the snow is icy or melted. Pretty bad condition, but still pretty fun.

Get close up to nature in 4K video and enjoy the virtual running video for treadmill run I uploaded.

Treadmill Virtual Running Adventures and virtual scenic for treadmill are filmed in 4K video. For those who want some adventure from the comforts of home or cannot get outdoors, or simply just want some motivation to get outside and explore.  virtual run and treadmill running video are videos watch while running treadmill of trail running, road running, mountain climbing and other beautiful landscapes. Videos to watch while running on treadmill with treadmill running scenery videos /scenery for treadmill will be uploaded every week if it's possible.

**Viewers are strongly discouraged from using VRA videos for treadmill while on exercise equipment such as a treadmill or similar device. Please use at your own risk.**

videos.title: Snowmobiling in Gaspésie | Simply Extraordinary.
videos.description:
Winter in Gaspésie is all about the joys of exploring backcountry trails by snowmobile… riding through a variety of sea and mountain landscapes in ideal snow conditions… and experiencing a profound feeling of freedom as you admire breathtaking views. Visit Gaspésie to soak up the extraordinary all around you as well as find it in the simple pleasures of life.

To make sure you enjoy every moment of your trip, plan your stay at tourisme-gaspesie.com.

And for more inspiring tips and ideas, follow us on social media!

► Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/tourismegaspesie 
► Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/tourismegaspesie. 
► Pinterest: https://www.pinterest.ca/tourismgaspesie 
► Site web: https://www.tourisme-gaspesie.com/en

videos.title: Centre de Ski Pin Rouge Gaspésie Quebec Canada  2021
videos.description:
Gaspésie, la station Pin Rouge dessert principalement les villes côtières de la région. Son grand chalet moderne de bois, érigé sur trois étages, est ceint d’une longue terrasse, tandis qu’au troisième étage une grande baie vitrée s’ouvre sur un balcon-terrasse plus intime. À l’intérieur, tout respire l’efficacité mise au service de la clientèle. Dans la grande cafeteria éclairée par le soleil, vous pourrez vous restaurer en regardant les skieurs dévaler les pentes, car face au chalet se dresse la montagne ! Et quelle montagne !

Le moins que l’on puisse dire, c’est que les 450 mètres de dénivelé de cette montagne ne sont pas destinés aux coeurs tendres! L’unique piste verte qui part du sommet, voit d’abord son tracé suivre le bord d’un long escarpement et la clôture qui sépare les glisseurs du vide doit paraître bien intimidante pour un débutant. Par contre, si vous aimez la vitesse et les sensations fortes, alors c’est l’endroit tout indiqué. D’ailleurs l’équipe de course qui y réside produit des coureurs pour lesquels, toutes les autres conditions rencontrées ailleurs doivent pâlir par comparaison.

La montagne, qui ne jouit pas d’un enneigement naturel très prononcé, s’est dotée de canons à neige et offre des surfaces très rapides et largement techniques. Pour ce qui est des pistes, vous trouverez une vaste gamme de pistes intermédiaires, difficiles et expertes. Et par expertes, nous entendons des pistes doubles losanges très inclinées qui mettront vos compétences à rude épreuve.

Parmi les expertes, le Mur et le Couloir offrent des bosses bien carabinées, tandis que la Zig-Zag, le Coude et la Village sont de longues pistes inclinées propices au carving de formule 1. Bien que courtes, le Défi, la Parade et la Cascade offrent de bons défis.

Côté sous-bois, la station a défriché deux sous-bois de calibre difficile, la Cascade et la Jongleuse, et un sous-bois de catégorie bleue, la Tue-mouches. Une bonne sélection de pistes bleues attend les intermédiaires et ils ne manqueront pas de terrain où s’amuser.

Une montagne capable de produire de grandes pointures comme François Bourque, saura sûrement vous offrir tous les défis dont vous rêvez. Et si vous ne rêvez pas de ski, la station offre également des glissades sur tubes, une patinoire ainsi que des sentiers de raquettes et de ski de fond.



Located in the Bay of Chaleur, near New Richmond on the south coast of the Gaspé Peninsula, Pin Rouge station serves mainly coastal towns in the region. Its large modern timber chalet, built on three floors, is surrounded by a long terrace, while on the third floor a large bay window opens onto a more intimate balcony-terrace. Inside, everything breathes efficiency put at the service of customers. In the large cafeteria lit by the sun, you can eat by watching the skiers hit the slopes, because facing the cottage stands the mountain! And what a mountain!

The least we can say is that the 450 meters of elevation of this mountain are not intended for tender hearts! The only green trail that starts from the summit, first sees its course follow the edge of a long escarpment and the fence that separates the sliders from the void must seem daunting for a beginner. On the other hand, if you like speed and thrills, then this is the place. Moreover, the race team that resides there produces runners for whom all the other conditions encountered elsewhere must pale by comparison.



The mountain, which does not enjoy a very pronounced natural snow, has equipped with snow cannons and offers very fast and largely technical surfaces. As for the slopes, you will find a wide range of intermediate tracks, difficult and expert. And by experts, we hear double sloping diamond tracks that will put your skills to the test.

Among the experts, the Wall and Corridor offer well carabinated bumps, while the Zig-Zag, Elbow and Village are long inclined trails conducive to carving Formula 1. Although short, the Challenge, the Parade and the Cascade offer good challenges.

On the undergrowth side, the station has cleared two difficult-sized undergrowths, Cascade and Juggler, and a blue-scale undergrowth, the Fly-catcher. A good selection of blue runs awaits the middlemen and they will have plenty of fun.

A mountain capable of producing big names like François Bourque, will surely offer you all the challenges you dream of. And if you do not dream of skiing, the resort also offers tube sliding, an ice rink and snowshoeing and cross-country skiing trails.

videos.title: Ep. 365: Gaspésie - Mountains and Sea | Quebec Canada RV travel camping hiking
videos.description:
We've traveled five hours from our last stop outside of Québec City to Québec's Gaspé Peninsula, known as Gaspésie in the native French language, where the St. Lawrence River widens to form the Gulf of St. Lawrence as it meets the Atlantic Ocean. This is also the northern end of the Appalachian Mountain chain where it terminates in the Chic-Choc and McGerrigle Mountains. While camping seaside at Camping au Bord de la mer, we'll explore the bountiful coastline here and go hiking in the spectacular Chic-Chocs. Come along!

Filmed: June 9-15, 2024

CHAPTERS

0:00 Intro
0:44 Gaspésie
2:18 Matane
3:43 Camping au Bord de la mer
8:04 Cap-Chat & Sainte-Anne-des-Monts
11:02 Gaspésie National Park
15:51 Hiking the Chic-Choc Mountains
20:44 Wrap-up and what's next

FOR MORE INFORMATION

* Our campsite https://maps.app.goo.gl/JbtoPdYdbbdVLbaVA
* Camping au Bord de la mer https://campingauborddelamer.com/
* Bonjour Québec https://www.bonjourquebec.com/
* Tourisme Gaspésie https://www.tourisme-gaspesie.com/
* Parc National de la Gaspésie https://www.sepaq.com/pq/gas
* Hiking Mont Ernest-Laforce https://www.alltrails.com/explore/trail/canada/quebec/sentier-du-mont-ernest-laforce

GET GRAND ADVENTURE STUFF

We've created some fun and quirky merchandise particularly for RV campers, including t-shirts, coffee mugs and more. Sales help to support Grand Adventure to keep bringing you outdoor adventure videos each and every week. Visit https://teespring.com/stores/grandadventure to browse our selection.

USE WHAT WE USE

We've opened a Grand Adventure store on Amazon! https://www.amazon.com/shop/GrandAdventure Unlike other Amazon shops, every single item you see in our store is hand picked from things we use ourselves in our own Grand Adventures, from RV stuff to our dogs and even photography and videography. We've tried it, we've tested it, and we can heartily recommend it. No random stuff or paid product placements.

It costs you no more to shop Amazon with us, but by shopping our store a portion of each sale goes to bringing you more and better Grand Adventure videos! Thank you for clicking through to give us a look!

CREDITS

🎵 We license the music for our videos from two different sources:

* Soundstripe https://soundstripe.com/?fpr=grandadventure . Use the coupon code "GRANDADVENTURE" to get 10% off a yearly or monthly Soundstripe subscription.

* Epidemic Sound https://share.epidemicsound.com/izovs4

ABOUT US

Welcome to Grand Adventure, a YouTube channel that focuses on RV-centric outdoor activities including not only of course camping, but also mountain biking, hiking, kayaking, skiing and more, always filmed in stunning 4K.

We're based in Salt Lake City, Utah during the winter months, and invite you to come along as we travel all around North America. My wife Patricia and I, and dog Zoe are avid boondockers, so while you'll occasionally find us in RV parks or even dry campgrounds, we're usually camped in some of the most remote and beautiful spots that you could ever pull a travel trailer into. We've now transitioned from "long-timing" for six months a year, to living and traveling in our RV full-time.

We'll provide beautiful travel videos, as well as some tips and insight on trip planning, and equipping and maintaining your RV to make your next adventure a grand one indeed! So, subscribe to make sure that you catch every episode, and remember...life is nothing but a Grand Adventure!

CONNECT WITH US

Become one of our Grand Adventurers and subscribe to our YouTube channel! 
Our website: http://GrandAdventure.tv
Like us! https://facebook.com/GrandAdventureRV/
Follow us! https://www.instagram.com/grand_adventure_rv/
Follow us! https://twitter.com/GrandAdventRV
Follow us! https://mewe.com/p/grandadventure
Find us! https://www.rvillage.com/group/2781/grand-adventure

videos.title: The Best Places to visit in Canada - Gaspésie Peninsula (Discover Canada series)
videos.description:
+ Dive  into the heart of Canada's breathtaking landscapes with "The Best Places to Visit in Canada - Gaspésie Peninsula," a captivating episode from the "Discover Canada" documentary series, now available on YouTube. This visually stunning film takes you on an unforgettable journey to the Gaspésie Peninsula, a hidden gem located in the eastern part of Quebec that boasts an unparalleled blend of natural beauty, cultural richness, and adventurous activities.
                                                                                  
+ From  the majestic Percé Rock rising out of the Gulf of Saint Lawrence to the lush, rugged interior of the Chic-Choc Mountains, the documentary showcases the peninsula's most iconic sights through mesmerizing drone footage, intimate wildlife encounters, and interviews with local residents and experts who share their deep connection with the land and its history.
                                                                                    
+ Perfect for travel enthusiasts, nature lovers, and anyone curious about Canada's diverse landscapes, "The Best Places to Visit in Canada - Gaspésie Peninsula" offers a comprehensive and inspiring guide to one of Canada's most spectacular regions.

of

background the celebrated Route 132 is

when the British conquered Canada they

during the summer it is a river where

the structure features an observation

Bay and bathing boating and other

home thanks to its Serene transparent

but capshat isn't just about mountains

various colors can truly be found
sometime ago one person painted their
house yellow another painted it green
and yet another painted it red there are
even purple houses people might think
it's due to bad taste or poverty however
that's not the case the fishermen would
venture far out on the river and when it
was time to return home they navigated
towards their respective colored houses
particularly here in capshat fishermen
returning from their fishing trips would
look for their House's distinctive color
this is why you can find houses of all
kinds of colors in gaspy today these
colors are used for folkloric purposes
witness to the river's history the only

estuaries the current Lighthouse

nestled between the Sea and the

earliest

because we are at the border between the

to fulfill our mission of helping you

here I have a female in order to

abdomen the abdomen of the male
resembles a folded tail it is always
very narrow and pointed much like a neck
tie the female's abdomen is slightly

so that is how we differentiate between
the male and female rock
crab St endemont is indeed a maritime
City
every year after the winter there are

Lawrence at explorer was launched the

spread across a vast expanse of over 800

age-old ice and
stone here the shikshak mountains which

animals and since the surrounding

shooting can also be indulged
dominating the mountain peaks is in
Alpine Tundra that serves as home and
protector to the endangered wood
Caribou nestled into gaspy park lies in
another Treasure of the gasby peninsula
Mount ly zagat's
mine in the open pit mine visit visitors
can pick geodes containing such
semi-precious stones as agots amethyst
quartz Carnelian or
Jasper equipped with some tools and a
good pair of shoes enthusiasts can spend
a whole day prospecting
the pleasure in this activity lies in
discovering our Treasures it is
enjoyable the two sites I will show you
are full of these Stones geodes what is
very interesting is that there is never
the same thing inside let's climb up
right away I'll show you where these
stones are
the fun begins even before arriving at

differences to detect in order to find
the most beautiful stones in the mine
due to the richness of this site it is
impossible to leave empty-handed at the
end there is the stone cutter who is an

stones and determine which of them have
more potential every stone is guaranteed
here if there are stones with no gems
inside they will be simply replaced by
us here at Mont ly the gems are not
lacking continuing to roll between sea
and mountains along Route 132 just 25 km
east of St and deont travelers encounter

Mar with a breathtaking view from its

gasby coast stands proudly on a natural
Promontory and so the beauty of the
place is whole there it is close to the
lighthouse the local church St Mark the
only one in gasby being covered with
cedar
shingles the four keepers of the
lighthouse spent altogether nearly 100
years here they were privileged to live
in the heart of the village due to the
location of the
lighthouse thus its nickname the
Cadillac of lighthouses as the keepers
never had to deal with the loneliness
that usually characterizes their
profession the Lighthouse of law marker
is the only one in the province that
still uses the original weight driven
mechanical system like the old clocks
used to have in order to rotate its
lighting module
this is the light source module only the
red painted part weighs 2 tons the way
it operates is quite impressive because
there are no ball bearings inside they
weren't used at that time as they
weren't so welldeveloped in terms of
mechanics this system floats on a bath
of mercury in other words the bottom of
this tank is filled with Mercury around
10 L this is whatat creates the space
Mercury is dense enough to support the
total weight of the
module it's fral lens a masterpiece of
Lighthouse engineering has cast its
light over 30 nautical miles into the
Gulf for over a century guiding ships
safely to
Harbor shck fast
each Lighthouse has its own light code
here it is four flashes and a pause of
30 seconds we can clearly see the four
sections that create flashes these round
sections that amplify the flow of light
this is the pause section the light does
not get through each Lighthouse is very
different from another so the module is
also different for example at mateline
in the next Village the code is three
flashes
so there were several variables that
could be adjusted to change the code and
to assign each site a specific
code the Lighthouse of lawn marker is an
exemplary piece of our Maritime
history with its signature Red Cap it is
not only a functional nautical Landmark
